gene_region.label: HFE transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical HFE genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: HFE encodes an MHC class I-like iron-regulatory protein that interacts with transferrin receptor biology and contributes to hepcidin-mediated systemic iron balance.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local HFE bundle is hereditary hemochromatosis and iron-metabolism oriented. Penetrance is incomplete and interpretation depends on genotype combination, sex, ferritin/transferrin saturation, ancestry, and clinical context.
variant_effect_overview: C282Y and H63D are common HFE markers with genotype-combination-dependent hemochromatosis relevance.
Single heterozygous findings often have limited predictive value without iron studies or compound genotype context.
condition_research_overview: Transferrin receptor and iron homeostasis biology
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: HFE methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for HFE.
Because HFE biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.
Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use HFE promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000010704: HFE coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: HFE biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis
recommended_icons: Vessel cross-section with red  blood cells (anemic)
Blood vessel (with red  blood cells, anemic)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an HFE iron-homeostasis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, iron overload susceptibility, or hereditary hemochromatosis review. Penetrance and clinical meaning depend on genotype combination and iron studies.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1800562 (HFE C282Y) is curated as a HFE marker for Hereditary hemochromatosis review context, Iron overload and hepcidin regulation. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag iron sensing, transferrin receptor interaction, hepcidin regulation, and systemic iron homeostasis.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Transferrin receptor and iron homeostasis biology
research_context: Use this marker as a HFE pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs1800562 (HFE C282Y) with HFE methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
